seoTitle: How to Import Contacts in Brevo
metaDescription: Migrate your subscriber database into Brevo cleanly, from structuring CSV files to mapping columns and monitoring the processing queue.
intro: Migrating an audience from an older CRM or launching a new newsletter list involves more than a simple file upload. Brevo's contact management interface uses smart attribute recognition to scan and pair your data columns, but getting clean results still depends on how well you structure the source file first. This guide covers the full import process.

steps.heading: Structure your spreadsheet
steps.body: Organize your Excel or CSV file with clear headers to ensure file uploads process smoothly without data drops.

steps.heading: Upload through the audience control panel
steps.body: Navigate to the centralized audience control panel and start the import process with your prepared file.

steps.heading: Map columns to profile fields
steps.body: Match unassigned file columns, such as email, name, and phone numbers, with your custom marketing database fields.

steps.heading: Assign tags for categorization
steps.body: Tag the imported contacts appropriately so they're easy to segment and target later.

steps.heading: Verify compliance and monitor the queue
steps.body: Confirm legal opt-in compliance for your imported list, then monitor the database processing queue until the import finishes.

faq.question: What file types can I import into Brevo?
faq.answer: Standard CSV or TXT spreadsheets are typically used, with clean headers helping the system auto-recognize your data columns.

faq.question: Will Brevo automatically detect my column headers?
faq.answer: Brevo's data mapping engine attempts to automatically scan and pair headers like email, name, and phone numbers, though you can adjust mappings manually if needed.

faq.question: Can I avoid creating duplicate contacts during import?
faq.answer: Structuring clean, unique data in your source file before uploading helps minimize duplicate profiles.

faq.question: How do I know when my import is finished?
faq.answer: You can monitor the database processing queue in Brevo to track the status of your import until it completes.
